title: Start pod infra container when pod is created
body:
When we create a pod that also has an infra container, we should
start the infra container automatically.  This allows users to add
running containers to the pod immediately.

Signed-off-by: baude <bbaude@redhat.com>

comments.body:
Does this mean the pause container gets launched when I do 

podman pod create

as opposed to having to do 
podman pod start -l

After the pod was created?

@rhatdan Yes.

We should probably reference CRI-O to see when they start theirs.
It looks like CRI-O starts the infra container at pod creation - like this PR would do. I think we're good to merge here.
